message:
feat(http): injectable time source for Http1Listener stats and lifecycle

Replace SystemTime::now()-based diagnostics in Http1ListenerStats with
an injectable fn() -> Time, propagated through Http1ListenerConfig:

- Http1ListenerStats gains a time_getter field used by record_accepted
  to stamp last_accept_at_ms with logical runtime time instead of
  system wall clock, enabling deterministic testing of listener stats.
- Http1ListenerConfig gains a time_getter builder method, defaulting
  to default_listener_time_getter which reads from Cx::current()
  timer_driver when available, falling back to wall_now.
- Http1Listener::bind and from_listener pass the configured time_getter
  through to ShutdownSignal::with_time_getter, ConnectionManager::
  with_time_getter, and Http1ListenerStats::new.
- transient_accept_now() now delegates to default_listener_time_getter
  for consistency.
- Manual Debug impl for Http1ListenerStats (replacing derive) since
  fn pointers don't impl Debug.

New test: configured_time_getter_controls_listener_bookkeeping verifies
that a test-controlled AtomicU64-backed time source drives stats
timestamps, connection metadata, and shutdown drain deadlines.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
